notes: = 10.83 mL/s at zero back-pressure (deadhead-free); catalogue 'max flow rate'
community 'water debit' figure for a vibe pump; ~8 mL/s is the practical unrestricted flow, lower than catalogue 10.8 (in-machine losses)
catalogue max pressure at zero flow (fully stalled); OPV normally caps working system far below this
1 min ON / 1.5 min OFF rated; DE1 uses P500-class ~2/1 with lower duty stress (dual-pump waveform control)
nominal espresso brew pressure; where OPV or DE1 pressure target usually sits
vibe pump flow falls with back-pressure; NOT a clean quadratic. Stroke shortens as backpressure rises (compression stroke unfinished before next current pulse) -> nonlinear droop
ONLY the two endpoints are manufacturer-stated. Interior shape is concave (droops faster near stall). Do NOT interpolate linearly.

DE1 ESP32 firmware carries a physics model of the pump giving flow from pressure+voltage+freq. Model is voltage/frequency dependent and country-calibrated; coefficients not published. de1app (Tcl, open) receives estimates, does not contain the pump curve.
the ONE measured machine-stage quadratic already in repo; single rig; this G3 drop adds the Ulka physical envelope + DE1 shape context, not a second rig
